ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for reduced-sugar hot chocolate premix; SNOMED CT coverage for commercial beverage mixes is limited.

preparation_method: dissolve in hot water or milk

serving.description: 1 sachet or 3 teaspoons (approx. 20g) dissolved in 150ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 20g of premix powder (one sachet or three heaped teaspoons) dissolved in 150ml of hot water, yielding a 170ml cup of hot chocolate.

nutrient_source.title: Hot chocolate (less sugar, premix) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Hot chocolate (less sugar, premix)', CR ID D04020011. All per-100g values are as reported by HPB. Saturated fat not separately reported by HPB for this entry; set to 0.0 consistent with total fat of 0.0g per HPB. Calcium and iron reported as 0.0 by HPB for this entry.

gi_source.title: Cocoa-based instant drink mix, reduced sugar — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for chocolate-flavoured milk and cocoa drink mixes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for this specific HPB-listed 'Hot chocolate (less sugar, premix)' product. GI of 40 is estimated by analogy with cocoa-based instant drink mixes and reduced-sugar chocolate drink powders documented in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 35–45 for similar products). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 0.922g per 20g serving) / 100 ≈ 0.4. This is a low-GL food per serving. The input record cited 'Embedded GI Database' with confidence 0.397, which is not an approved source; the analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database is substituted here.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar and sodium content in a sweetened instant premix beverage
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this sweetened instant premix drink due to its added sugar (4.1g/100g) and moderate sodium (87mg/100g); cumulative intake across multiple servings per day can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium load, and the absence of compensating nutrients such as potassium or dietary fibre reduces its dietary value for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and high-GI maltodextrin in a sweetened instant premix classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product: despite the 'less sugar' label, it contains added sugar (4.1g/100g) and likely maltodextrin (GI ~110), which can cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation; unsweetened cocoa powder dissolved in plain low-fat milk is a clinically preferable alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar content and ultra-processed formulation with no beneficial lipid or fibre profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should minimise consumption of this ultra-processed sweetened premix, as its added sugar contributes to glycaemic load and triglyceride elevation, while its zero dietary fibre and zero beneficial fats provide no cardiovascular protective benefit; plain cocoa in unsweetened beverages is preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate (maltodextrin) and zero anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this premix as its maltodextrin content promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, it contains zero dietary fibre and zero calcium (a concern for those on corticosteroids), and the reduced cocoa content in the premix formulation is unlikely to deliver meaningful polyphenol anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hot Chocolate (Less Sugar, Premix) is a commercially formulated powdered chocolate drink mix designed to be dissolved in hot water or milk. It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage premix and is listed in the Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient database (CR ID D04020011) under the food subgroup 'Malted and chocolate drinks'. The powder typically contains cocoa powder, reduced quantities of sugar, maltodextrin, and milk solids, yielding a warm chocolate-flavoured beverage when reconstituted. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the product retains added sugar at 4.1g per 100g and is therefore classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore's dietary assessment frameworks. The energy content is low at 26 kcal per 100g as prepared, with 1.53g protein, 0g fat, and 4.61g carbohydrate. Sodium is moderate at 87mg per 100g. Dietary fibre is 0g. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low GI) by analogy with similar cocoa-based drink mixes; however, the presence of maltodextrin — a refined starch with a very high GI of approximately 110 — means the product may cause faster postprandial glucose elevation than the overall GI estimate suggests. Clinically, this product is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its added sugar, absence of fibre, and ultra-processed formulation. It is also of concern for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its pro-inflammatory refined carbohydrate content and absence of calcium. A serving is typically one sachet (approximately 20g) dissolved in 150ml hot water.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D04020011, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database due to absence of a direct measurement for this specific product, which reduces GI confidence to medium; however, overall record confidence remains high given the exact HPB nutrient match.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database for this product formulation; saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with total fat of 0.0g as reported by HPB. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB. Calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 by HPB for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product and are not available from other approved sources for this specific premix formulation. The input record listed 'Embedded GI Database' as the GI source with confidence 0.397; this is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2 and has been replaced with an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. The input dietary_tags included 'keto', which is not appropriate for a product containing 4.61g carbohydrate per 100g and added sugar; this tag has been removed. The input listed 'low_calorie' which is defensible at 26 kcal/100g as prepared. Multilingual local names have been added as this is a commercial product sold in Singapore, and thus requires Singaporean multilingual coverage.
